main_sections.title: What a Bird Thought
main_sections.poem_text:
I lived first in a little house,
And lived there very well,
I thought the world was small and round,
And made of pale, blue shell.

I lived next in a little nest,
Nor needed any other,
I thought the world was made of straw,
And nestled by my mother.

One day, I fluttered from my nest,
To see what I could find,
I said the world is made of leaves,
I have been very blind.

At length I flew beyond the tree,
Quite fit for grown-up labours,
I don't know how the world is made,
And neither do my neighbours.

Anonymous
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem traces a baby bird's growing understanding of its world across four stanzas, each representing a stage of life. In the egg (little house), the world is small, round, and pale blue — literally the shell itself. In the nest, the world is warm straw and the mother's presence. When the fledgling first ventures out, it sees leaves and realises with humility that it had 'been very blind'. Finally, flying beyond the tree as an adult, the bird discovers that the world is immense and unknowable — even its neighbours share this wonder. The poem's central theme is the expansion of perception with experience, and the wisdom that grows from acknowledging what one does not know. The tone moves from confidence through self-correction to philosophical acceptance. The dominant device is metaphor — the bird's stages map onto childhood, adolescence, and adulthood — with rhyme (well/shell, other/mother, find/blind, labours/neighbours) giving a gentle, song-like quality.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the poem again. Identify the main idea of each stanza. There are two extra sentences given.

question_bank.stem: Match each stanza of the poem to its main idea. Options (two are extra): (1) The bird steps out into the world. (2) The bird lived happily in a small world. (3) The bird becomes blind due to leaves. (4) The bird flies away as an adult. (5) The bird is unhappy with the straw nest. (6) The bird lived in a cosy nest with its mother.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look at the pictures 1–4 and write the matching line from the poem for each.

question_bank.stem: Match each picture (egg, nest with mother, bird on branch among leaves, bird flying away) to the correct line from the poem.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer the following with a word from the poem and check the answers with your partner.

question_bank.stem: What was the shape of the bird's first house? _ O _ _ _
What was the bird's second nest made of? _ _ R _ _
What did the bird see when it came out of its nest? L _ _ _ E _
What did the bird do at the end? _ _ _ W

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Now, recite the poem aloud along with your teacher and classmates.
Write whether the following sentences are True or False.

question_bank.stem: The bird changed its house two times.
The neighbours tell the bird about the world.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines from the poem and answer the questions given below. (1) 'I thought the world was made of straw, / And nestled by my mother.'

question_bank.stem: Who lived in the nest along with the baby bird?

Complete the following by choosing the correct option. The bird thought the world was made of straw because __________. A. it lived in a straw hut  B. its nest was made of straw  C. its mother fed straw to it  D. there was straw all around
options: it lived in a straw hut
its nest was made of straw
its mother fed straw to it

question_bank.stem: Which word tells us that the baby bird was warm and comfortable?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines from the poem and answer the questions given below. (2) 'I said the world is made of leaves, / I have been very blind.'

question_bank.stem: Why did the bird think the world was made of leaves?
What does the set of words 'I said' mean? A. flew  B. shouted  C. spoke  D. cried
Fill in the blanks to complete the sentence. The bird felt that it had been very blind because it could not __ __ __ the leaves from __ n __ __ __ e the nest.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Why did the baby bird think that its first world was small, round and blue?

question_bank.stem: Why did the baby bird think that its first world was small, round and blue?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Where did the bird go when it fluttered from its straw nest?

question_bank.stem: Where did the bird go when it fluttered from its straw nest?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: What quality did the bird say it had, when it flew away?

question_bank.stem: What quality did the bird say it had, when it flew away?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Who do you think were the bird's neighbours? Why do you think so? Discuss.

question_bank.stem: Who do you think were the bird's neighbours? Why do you think so?

listening.transcript_text:
I'm a parrot, my name is Paro,
My feathers are bright and they glow.
In the morning, I spread my wings,
Fly around and see wonderful things.
Come with me through the skies so blue,
To places old and places new.
Among the trees, where the breeze is light,
Many new stories, oh, what a sight!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a short poem. As you listen, fill in the missing words. You can listen to the poem more than once.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the missing words as you listen to the poem about Paro the parrot: My feathers are __ __ __ g __ __ and they glow. | ___ ___ ___ around and see wonderful things. | Come with me through the skies so ___ l ___ ___. | Among the ___ ___ e ___ ___, where the breeze is light. | Many new s t __ __ __ ___ s, oh, what a sight!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Speak about which bird you would like to be. Include the following points: which bird are you [I am…], what do you look like [I am … in colour. I am a big/small bird. I have…], where do you live [I live in… It is (where)…], what do you do during the day [I… I also…], do you have any message for humans [I want to tell the humans…].

Read how the bird perceived its world [model paragraph provided]. Now, discuss with your teacher and classmates how you think the world would look to: (1) a baby, (2) a fish. Now, write five sentences each, for both 1 and 2, in your notebooks.

question_bank.stem: Write five sentences describing how the world looks to a baby.
Write five sentences describing how the world looks to a fish.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us write some rhyming words. Words from the poem are given for Stanza 1 (well, shell). Find rhyming words for all four stanzas and add new rhyming words.

question_bank.stem: Complete the rhyming words table for all four stanzas. Stanza 1: well, shell | Stanza 2: [other, mother] | Stanza 3: [find, blind] | Stanza 4: [labours, neighbours]. Add new rhyming words for each stanza.
Complete the new stanza with rhyming words at the end of each line: I fly high and I fly low, / But one thing I surely k __ __ __, / Whenever I wish to rest, / My little nest is the __ __ __t.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: The poet uses words like 'pale', 'blue', 'round', 'straw' and 'little' to describe the bird's nest. Read a few more describing words: pointed, green, thin, brown, small, wooden, hanging, round. Now, use the describing words given in the box to create six sentences based on the given picture.

question_bank.stem: Use the describing words (pointed, green, thin, brown, small, wooden, hanging, round) to create six sentences based on the picture of a birdhouse.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: The bird's first home was made of a shell. Later its home was among leaves and made of straw. (I) Look at your home carefully and make a list of all the materials it is made of. Why do you think our homes are not made of straw? If you had to own a home of your choice, what would it look like and what materials would you like to use? How is a home different from a house? Discuss with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: Make a list of all the materials your home is made of.
Why do you think our homes are not made of straw?
If you had to own a home of your choice, what would it look like and what materials would you like to use?
How is a home different from a house? Discuss.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: (II) Look at the different kinds of nests and homes the birds make. Have you seen any? Discuss these type of nests with your teacher and classmates. Look at the list of some workers who build houses. Find out the work they do and write, as shown in the example. You can think of other workers and add them too.

question_bank.stem: Complete the table: Workers and their work. Workers given: painter, electrician, plumber, carpenter, architect (add two more of your own). Example: mason — lays bricks and stones.
